demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility; nursing staff on site

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel like I'm drowning every time I try to sleep, and I have to sit bolt upright just to catch a breath.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last two days I can't lie flat at all, I keep waking up gasping for air, and my legs have blown up huge — the night staff are worried and wanted someone to look at me.

symptom_narrative.onset: breathlessness on exertion has crept up over about two weeks, but it got dramatically worse in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: two weeks of gradual decline, sharply worse for two days
symptom_narrative.character: breathless and puffed out; a smothering, drowning feeling when lying down
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: frightening — I feel like I can't get enough air unless I'm sitting straight up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my chest feels tight and heavy, and my legs are swollen and heavy
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worst at night — I wake up suddenly gasping; I now need three pillows just to breathe
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting bolt upright, hanging my legs over the edge of the bed
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down flat makes it much worse, and any small effort leaves me winded
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my legs and feet are badly swollen and it's spread up my thighs; I've put on a lot of weight quickly; I feel wiped out and can barely get from the bed to the chair
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had a heart condition for years and take fluid tablets for it, but I've never felt this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can only get from bed to chair with help now; I'm scared of falling on my night trips to the toilet because my legs are so heavy

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-standing heart condition that I take water tablets for

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in an aged-care facility; needs help with most activities; high falls risk, especially getting up to the toilet at night

objective_data_offered.timing: at the time of the call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading from the facility fingertip oximeter, read out by nursing staff

objective_data_offered.timing: at the time of the call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility cuff, single reading reported by staff

objective_data_offered.timing: at the time of the call

objective_data_offered.value: 82 kg — up about 5 kg from her usual weight
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility weight log; baseline from prior charted weight

objective_data_offered.value: sitting bolt upright on the edge of the bed, breathing fast and hard, using her neck and shoulder muscles to breathe, speaking in short broken sentences
both legs and feet visibly swollen with the swelling extending well up the thighs
three pillows stacked on the bed; legs dangling dependently over the edge
staff report the last three days of her regular water tablet were missed because of a prescription refill error at the facility
